primary_sources.title: Event Timing API Editor's Draft
observations: User agents expose Event Timing support through PerformanceObserver.supportedEntryTypes.
The default event duration threshold is 104 ms; an observer can request a lower threshold, but the effective minimum is 16 ms.
first-input entries are reported regardless of durationThreshold and have distinct buffering behavior.
interactionId groups related event entries but is not a sequential interaction counter.

primary_sources.title: Performance Timeline Editor's Draft
observations: Runtime support must be detected rather than assumed across user agents.

finding: R-012 replay-window integrity is insufficient when a browser lacks Event Timing, first-input, or interaction grouping support, or when collection claims a threshold below the specification minimum. A capability packet must be validated before replay evidence is classified.
design_implication: Run event-timing-capability.mjs before bounded-replay-window.mjs. Treat unsupported entry types, unavailable interaction grouping, malformed thresholds, or thresholds below 16 ms as unresolved rather than as zero-latency evidence.

coverage: missing eventCounts preserved as an explicit limitation
raw event names, targets, selectors, and entries excluded

evidence_strength: direct local deterministic test plus current primary specification
uncertainty: Actual Safari and iPhone support must be measured at runtime.
eventCounts is treated as a completeness cross-check rather than a hard requirement because browser support may differ.
A valid capability packet does not prove that a particular replay was captured completely or caused a visual outcome.
falsification_route: Run the gate on browsers with intentionally missing supportedEntryTypes and require unresolved output.
Request thresholds below 16 ms and require rejection.
Compare runtime capability packets against actual PerformanceObserver construction and delivered entries.
Demonstrate that a valid packet can still accompany incomplete replay data, preserving the distinction between capability and evidence truth.
